ety: inf. n. ii of نظم 'to join, string,' &c.
def: Composing (verses); stringing or threading (pearls, &c.); ordering, arranging.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تنظیم (tanzīm), from Arabic تَنْظِيم (tanẓīm).

ety: inf. n. ii of نظر 'to look' (at)
def: s.f. Beholding, contemplating; — likening, resembling.

ety: for A. تنزية tanziyat, inf. n. ii of نزو 'to overpower'
def: s.m. Inspiring with desire; desire; propensity.

s.m. Heating (metals, &c.); heat, fusion; proof, trial, assay; passion, wrath, rage; strength, power; speed; splendour, dignity (these five meanings are peculiar to the Persian tāv, which, as regards the first three, is related to the Sanskrit tavās); — turning or twisting (metals, orig. by heating); contortion, twist, coil, curl, roll, fold; a sheet of paper
subs: s.m. Name of a powder or substance which, mixed with gold or silver, prevents the detection of adulteration
(-ko), To heat; to anneal; to melt, fuze; — to twist (cord, &c.), to curl or twist (the mustache, &c.)
v.n. To be heated, be made red hot; to be overdone, be scorched or burnt; to be overheated (as the blood); to be inflamed, be enraged.

def: adv. Rapidly, fast, quick.

ety: tāʼo˚ = Prk. ताव(इ), or तावे(इ) = S. तापय(ति), caus. of rt. तप्
def: v.t. = tānā, q.v. s.m. The threads that are extended lengthwise upon a loom, the warp
subs: v.n. To fidget, to dance attendance
(-ke), To set or dispose the warp (of a piece of cloth); to weave; — to wander hither and thither without profit; to plan and exert oneself to no purpose.

def: s.m. Warmth, heat, burning; fever (com. tap); affliction, pain, sorrow, distress; passion
subs: s.f. Induration or enlargement of the spleen attended with or preceded by fever; splenitis, ague-cake ( = tāb-tillī)
s.m. Remover of sorrow or distress, an epithet of a deity.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit ताप (tāpá), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *tāpás, from Proto-Indo-European *tep-.

ety: Pehl. tap; Zend tafnu, rt. tap; S. तप, or ताप
def: s.f. Heat, warmth; fever; — tap-utarnā, v.n. Fever to go off
subs: s.f. Induration and enlargement of the spleen, &c. = tāp-tiliī, q.v.s.v. tāp
s.m. Breaking out on the lips after a fever, or of a fever in the system; the thrush
s.m. The breaking out of an eruption on the lips after a fever

def: s.m. Devout austerity, religious penance, &c. ( = tapas, q.v.); the hot season, the height of summer
subs: adj. Depending on the merit of penance
s.m. One who depends for salvation on the merit of his deeds of penance

def: s.f. Hoof (of a horse); stroke (of a horse's forefoot), pawing; sound (of a horse's hoof, or of beating, &c.), tramp (of a horse); tap, whack; patting, stroking, feeling (cf. thāp); — a bamboo frame for catching fish ( = ṭāpā)
subs: (or ṭāp-ā-ṭoʼiyā) karnā, v.n. To feel about (for), to grope, to rummage; to trifle; to find out and repair (leaks in a thatched roof)
v.n. To paw or beat the ground (a horse), &c. = ṭāpnā, q.v.

def: s.m. Covering, cover, the upper part or fly (of a tent); the hood (of a carriage, &c.); sound of dropping (as of rain, &c.); dropping, patter, drop (of rain, &c.); — spring, leap, bound
subs: ṭap-ṭap, adv. With a sound like that of a dropping liquid; drop-drop, patter-patter; in continuous drops, in a shower (as rain, tears, bullets, &c.); one after another, continuously, successively.

def: s.m. The hot season, a season of great heat ( = tap, q.v.); — a devotee, ascetic, &c. ( = tapasī, tapasvī, q.v.).
s.m. = tappā; ṭappā, q.v.

s.m. A bell-shaped covering of bamboo-work for placing over fowls; a hen-coop; a bamboo frame for catching fish ( = ṭāp); a framework covered with net or muslin placed over a cradle; a framework cover generally (with or without any external covering; and, locally, anything with such a cover, as a hut, a boat or raft, &c.; also a covering of cloth, &c.; cf. ṭap).
s.m. (local), An island in a river (cf. ṭāpū).
s.m. = tapāk (the more com. form, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. Heating, burning, inflaming; — fever, morbid heat.
s.f. Sound of dropping (of a liquid, &c.); dropping, dripping; drop; beat, pulsation, throbbing, throb, pain
subs: s.m. One who reports erroneously what he pretends to have overheard.

def: s.m. Warmth, ardour, fervour, zeal; the anguish of love; solicitude of friendship; love, affection, friendship; apparent cordiality; — affliction, distress, uneasiness, disquietude; consternation; palpitation ( = tapak).
s.f. Throb, palpitation (cf. tapāk).
s.m. An officer formerly employed in revenue affairs.

ety: tapak˚ = S. तप+कृ; or = thapak˚, q.v.
def: v.n. To throb, to palpitate.
v.n. To drop (as a liquid, or as ripe fruit); to drip, dribble, distil, exude, leak; to beat, throb, palpitate; to ache, pain (cf. tapaknā)
subs: v.n. To drip; to drop off or down (as ripe fruit, &c.).

def: s.m. Heating, warming, burning, inflaming; distressing; the sun; name of a division of hell.

ety: imperf. part. of tapīdan, 'to tremble,' &c.; rt. Zend. tap = S. तप्
def: adj. Growing hot; becoming agitated; palpitating; trembling.
s.f. Heat, warmth, burning, glow, fervour; — s.m. The sun; the hot season; name of one of the divisions of hell; mental distress, grieving, pining.
part. adj. = تپان tapān, q.v. s.m. Heating, warming, burning, inflaming; distressing; the sun; name of a division of hell.

ety: tapā˚ = Prk. तवावे(इ) = S. तप+आपय˚ caus. augment
def: v.t. To heat, to warm; to cause to glow; to test (metals) by means of fire; to make (a person) warm himself (at the fire or in the sunshine).

ety: tap˚ = S. तर्पय˚ (caus. of rt. तृप्)+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.t. To present a libation to (the gods or to the manes); to present an offering.
v.t. To cause to jump or leap over; to cause to bound, &c.
s.f. A name of the Godavery river, or of the Tāpī.
adj. & s.m. Performing penance, practising austerities; devout; — a practiser of religious austerities, a devotee, ascetic, hermit.

ety: S. तपस्; or S. ताप+ās = S. वाञ्छा
def: s.f. Sunshine, the sunbeams; labour, toil.

s.m. Heat; fire (esp. as an instrument of self-torture); pain, suffering, torture; religious austerity, penance mortification, the practice of mental or personal self-denial or the infliction of bodily torture; the meditation connected with such a practice; — moral virtue, merit; special observance or duty of any particular caste (e.g. the tapas of a Brāhman is sacred learning; of a Kshatriya, the protection of subjects; of a Vaiśya, giving alms to Brāhmans; of a Śūdra, service; and of a Rishi or saint, feeding upon herbs and roots); one of the seven worlds, inhabited by saints or devotees after death; — the hot season
subs: s.m. The power acquired by religious austerities
adj. & s.m. Rich in religious penance; ascetic, pious; — one who treasures up the merit of mortifications and austerities, an ascetic, a devotee, one who performs religious penance
s.m. One of the seven worlds (see above)
s.m. 'Enclosure or district of religious penance,' a name applied to the holy land situated in central India
tapo-ban, s.m. 'Penance grove,' a sacred grove in which ascetics perform their religious austerities

def: s.f. corr. of tapasyā, q.v. s.f. Devout austerity, religious penance.
adj. & s.m. = tapasvī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Engaged in the practice of rigorous and devout penance; ascetic, devout; — an ascetic, devotee, a jogī.
adj. & s.m. = tapasya, q.v.
subs: tapsī-maććhī, tapsī-maćh, s.f. The mango fish, Polynemus risua or Paradiseus.

def: = H تپشيا तपश्या tapaśyā, see tapassī, and tapasyā.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Engaged in the practice of rigorous and devout penance; ascetic, devout; — an ascetic, devotee, a jogī.
ex: یہ تپسوی جنگل میں رہتے ہیں۔
ye tapasvī jaṅgal mẽ rahte ha͠i.
These ascetics live in the forest.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit तपस्वी (tapasvī).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Engaged in the practice of rigorous and devout penance; ascetic, devout; — an ascetic, devotee, a jogī.
s.f. Devout austerity, religious penance.
adj. & s.m. Produced by heat; — the month Phāgun (Feb.-March); — religious penance, &c. ( = tapasyā).
= H تپشي तपश्शी tapaśśī, see tapassī, and tapasyā.

r: ṭapā-ṭoʼā, ṭappā-ṭoʼā, ṭapā-ṭoʼī, ṭappā-ṭoʼī, ṭapnā, ṭo-nā, ṭāpā-ṭoʼī, ṭāp
def: s.m. = H ٿپاٿوئي टपाटोई ṭapā-ṭoʼī, टप्पाटोईṭappā-ṭoʼī, s.f. (from ṭapnā and ṭo-nā), Feeling (for), groping, passing the hand over ( = ṭāpā-ṭoʼī, q.v.s.v. ṭāp).

r: ṭapā-ṭoʼī, ṭappā-ṭoʼī, ṭapā-ṭoʼā, ṭappā-ṭoʼā, ṭapnā, ṭo-nā, ṭāpā-ṭoʼī, ṭāp
def: s.f. = H ٿپاٿوا टपाटोआ ṭapā-ṭoʼā, टप्पाटोआṭappā-ṭoʼā, s.m. (from ṭapnā and ṭo-nā), Feeling (for), groping, passing the hand over ( = ṭāpā-ṭoʼī, q.v.s.v. ṭāp).
s.m. Pouring out a libation; a libation.
v.t. = tapānā, q.v. v.t. To heat, to warm; to cause to glow; to test (metals) by means of fire; to make (a person) warm himself (at the fire or in the sunshine).
adj. & s.m. Heated, glowing; oppressed by heat; suffering from disease (moral or physical); — anything heated or glowing.
s.f. Name of the Surāt river (also called Tāptī); — a name of the Jamnā river.
adj. & s.m. = tapasvī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Engaged in the practice of rigorous and devout penance; ascetic, devout; — an ascetic, devotee, a jogī.
part. (of t̤apīdan), Agitated, distressed (esp. with heat), heated; palpitating, fluttering.

ety: rt. Zend tap+tan; S. तप्+तं; with conjunct. ī
def: adj. & adv. Agitated, troubled; — in a troubled condition, in a state of agitation.
s.m. = tapīdan, q.v. adj. & adv. Agitated, troubled; — in a troubled condition, in a state of agitation.
s.f. Heat, warmth; distress (esp. that caused by heat); affliction; agitation; palpitation.
s.f. = tapish, q.v. s.f. Heat, warmth; distress (esp. that caused by heat); affliction; agitation; palpitation.
part. Heated, inflamed; distressed, pained.
s.m. Continuous dropping, dripping; a drop (of rain, &c., cf. ṭipkā); fruit falling when ripe (particularly mangoes), a windfall
subs: s.f. Continuous dropping, dripping, trickle; a continuous dropping or falling off (as of fruit, or customers, or of men or animals under epidemic or murrain)
(-kā), To drip; to become leaky, to leak.

def: v.t. To cause to drop or drip, to distil.

ety: rt. of ṭapkānā+āw = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. Dripping; distillation.
s.m. See s.v. tap. s.m. Warmth, heat, burning; fever (com. tap); affliction, pain, sorrow, distress; passion
subs: s.f. Induration or enlargement of the spleen attended with or preceded by fever; splenitis, ague-cake ( = tāb-tillī)
s.m. Remover of sorrow or distress, an epithet of a deity.

ety: ṭapak, q.v.+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Friskiness, skittishness
subs: (-kī), To become frisky or skittish, to become wanton (a woman).

ety: tāp˚ = S. तापय(ति), caus. of rt. तप्;+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S.अनीयं
def: v.t. To heat, warm, warm oneself (at or over a fire, e.g. āg tāpnā); — v.n. To bask (in the sun or before a fire).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀢𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀇 (tappaï), from Sanskrit तप्य॑ति (tápyati). Cognate with Punjabi ਤਪਣਾ (tapṇā) / تَپݨا (tapṇā), Sindhi تَپَڻُ (tapaṇu) / तपणु, Gujarati તાપવું (tāpvũ), Marathi तापणे (tāpṇe), Sinhalese තපිනවා (tapinawā).

ety: tap˚ = Prk. तप्प(इ) = S. तप(ति) or तप्य(ति), rt. तप्
def: v.n. To be heated; to glow; to burn with pain or grief; to frisk about; to shine, to be glorious.

v.n. To cover, to superpose; to put the hand (upon), pass the hand (over); to top, mount; to copulate, couple, pair (as birds, &c.); to jump (over or across), to vault; to jump about, be skittish, to frisk, gambol
subs: (-ko), To spring or vault (over), to leap or jump (over or across); to jump down (-meṅ, upon, or in the midst of); (fig.) to thrust oneself (into the concerns of another), to intermeddle, interfere

def: v.n. To paw or beat the ground (a horse); to beat the feet on the ground, to stamp (in impatience, or pain, or despair, &c.), to be impatient, restless, or agitated; to tramp about; — v.t. To leap or spring over (a wall, &c.), to scale ( = ṭapnā).

s.m. See s.v. tapas for compounds of which tapo is the first member. adj. & s.m. Performing penance, practising austerities; devout; — a practiser of religious austerities, a devotee, ascetic, hermit.

s.m. Spring, leap, jump (in measure); bound, range (of a ball, bullet, &c.); distance, intervening space, interval (cf. pallā); a long stitch (in sewing); a relay, post (of ḍāk-bearers, post-runners, &c.); station for relays, post-station, stage, halting or resting-place; a post office; a raft, a catamaran (cf. ṭāpā); a mode (in music); a kind of hook; a small tract or division of country (smaller than a pargana, and consisting, generally, of a large town with villages and lands dependent; — ṭappā ḍālnā (-meṅ), To put in long stitches, to use long stitches, to baste
subs: v.n. To bound or ricochet (a ball)
v.n. To bound; to make a long jump, take a long leap; to skip (in reading), to read in a desultory way; to baste ( = ṭappā ḍālnā).

def: s.m. = ٿپا ṭappā, q.v.
s.m. properly ṭappā, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) A post-station; post-office; post, mail; a relay (of runners, &c.)
subs: s.m. A runner, letter-carrier, postman ( = ṭappālī; ḍāk-wālā).

def: s.m. A letter-carrier, postman ( = ṭappāl-wālā).
s.m. A thatched house, a hut, &c. (see ṭāpā).

ety: tap˚ = thāp, q.v.+rī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A mound; a small eminence or hillock, an acclivity; a limitary dike ( = meṅṛ).
s.m. Plots of land (divided by means of taprīs), land cultivated in small patches among the uncultivated portions of an estate.
adj. = tapta and tattā, qq.v. part. adj. Heated, inflamed; hot, fervent, burning or burnt with heat, pain, or sorrow
subs: s.m. A kind of penance, drinking hot water, milk, or ghī for three days.

def: part. adj. Heated, inflamed; hot, fervent, burning or burnt with heat, pain, or sorrow
subs: s.m. A kind of penance, drinking hot water, milk, or ghī for three days.

def: v.n. To drop continually, to drip.
s.f. Heat, &c. (see tapish).
s.m. An island; a shoal ( = ćar).
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀝𑀸𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀼𑀓 (*ṭāppuka).

def: s.m. A mineral substance, sulphuret of iron (said to be obtained from the river Tāpī).

ety: S. तन्त्र; and cf. S. तारः
def: s.m. Thread, string; the warp or threads extended lengthwise in a loom; wire, cord, string of a musical instrument; uninterrupted series, line, continuation, succession; a letter-file; the electric telegraph ( = ṭār-barqī); a telegraphic message, telegram; a hair of a curl or ringlet; the hair-worm or guinea worm; the snout, sucking tube, or trunk of most winged insects; the antennæ or feelers of prawns, flies, &c.; a bit, piece
subs: (-ke), To continue or repeat (an act) without interruption
s.f. The electric wire, electric telegraph; a telegram
(-ke), To take place uninterruptedly or continuously (an act), to be continued or repeated without interruption
adv. Piece by piece, piecemeal; distinctly; in pieces or shreds, tattered and torn
v.n. To speak in detail or distinctly
(-ke), To tear to pieces or into shreds
(-ke), To be broken in continuity; to be interrupted; to be disjointed; to be separated
s.m. A kind of needlework, open work; pulling out some threads, &c.
s.f. A kind of needlework, any fine cloth run with gold or silver thread
s.m. A drawer of gold or silver wire
tār-kashī. s.f. Wire-drawing; a kind of needlework

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian تَار (tār). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi تار (tar /⁠tār⁠/).

def: s.m. The palmyra-tree or fan-palm, Borassus flabelliformis; the tree Corypha taliera (the leaves of which are used for writing upon)
subs: vulg. tāṛ-pattar, s.m. The palm-leaf prepared for writing on; a document written on palm-leaves
s.m. The gelatinous pulp of the fruit of the palmyra tree.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀸𑀟 (tāḍa), from Sanskrit ताड (tāḍa).

ety: Zend tauruna; S. taruṇ, rt. tr̤ī
def: adj. New, fresh; green; young, tender, soft; juicy, moist, damp, wet, wet through, saturated (with moisture, or grease, &c.); refreshed, revived, gladdened; — loose, large (as shoes, &c.)
subs: adj. Completely wet, saturated, drenched, soaking, dripping; covered (with blood)
s.m. A wet bandage (applied to a wound)
s.f. Application of a wet bandage (to a wound)
s.f. Activity, handiness, dexterity, expertness, adroitness
v.t. To wet; to steep, soak, to drench, saturate; — tar karne-wālā, adj. (in medicine) Demulcent
adj. Fresh and moist; ripe and fresh; quite fresh.

def: An affix, the termination of the comparative degree in Persian (and in Sanskrit).
s.m. Side, party, division, faction; — an imitative sound
subs: (S. taṭa-taṭa), s.m. & adv. The sound produced by a thing falling continuously or in quick successive strokes upon another, crack-crack, whack-whack, patter-patter; — in quick successive strokes, with a crack-crack, &c. (e.g. jūtiyāṅ taṛ-ā-taṛ paṛīṅ).

def: s.f. = ṭāl, q.v. s.m. Pond, pool, tank, lake ( = tālāb).

s.f. Shout, yell, scream, cry, croak (of a frog), chatter, clatter, clack (cf. ṭar-ṭar); shout of joy, rejoicing; a crane; — rudeness, roughness, insolence, &c. ( = ṭarrā-pan); — adj. Senseless, stupid, dull; inattentive, regardless (like one intoxicated); intoxicated.

postpn., for tal = tale, q.v. s.m. The palmyra-tree or fan-palm ( = tāṛ, q.v.); — clapping the hands together; slapping the hands on the arms in defiance (in wrestling = tāṛī, q.v.); beating time (in music); musical time or measure; chime; a musical instrument of bell-metal or brass, a sort of cymbal (played with a stick); a pair of cymbals; — the glass or pebble of an eye-glass
subs: (˚la+ākh˚), s.f. A sort of vegetable perfume ( = tāl-parṇī)
s.m. The palm-leaf ( = tāṛ-patra, q.v.); — a hollow cylinder of gold (or a palm-leaf used as a substitute), with or without a ring attached, and thrust through the lobe of the ear as an ornament
s.f. A sort of anise, Anethum graveolens; a kind of vegetable perfume
s.m. A medicinal application to the eyes
v.n. To slap the arms defiantly (in wrestling = tāṛī ṭhoṅknā)
adj. & s.m. Having legs as long as a palm-tree; — name of a Rākshas; of a Daitya; of certain princes
v.t. To chime; to slap the arms ( = tāl ṭhoṅknā)
v.n. To get, or to be, out of tune
s.m. The medicinal herb Barleria (or Asteracantha) longifolia; the seeds of Solanum indicum (see makhānā)
s.m. Harmony, agreement, accord, &c. ( = ittifāq, q.v.); — tāl-mel khānā, v.n. To be in harmony, to agree, &c. ( = ittifāq honā)
tāl-vr̤int, s.m. A palm-leaf used as a fan; a fan.

def: s.f. Understanding, perception, apprehension, cognizance
subs: adj. Of keen perception, quick of apprehension, intelligent
s.f. Quickness of apprehension, intelligence, sharpness.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀢𑀸𑀟 (tāḍa), from Sanskrit ताड (tāḍa).

subs: s.m. The path of the stars, the atmosphere, firmament, heaven or the sky
v.n. A star to shoot, a meteor to fall
v n A star or planet to set
s.m. 'Star-planet,' one of the five lesser planets (exclusive of the sun and moon)
s.m. The starry host, the stars
s.m. The starry region, the sphere of the stars, the firmament, sky, the visible heavens
v.n. To become as a star, to appear as small as a star; to attain to a great height, to reach or touch the sky
v.n. To impose (upon), to practise deceit
v.n. To see lights or stars dancing before the eyes (as after a knock, or through debility, &c.)
tāre ugnā, v.n. = tāre nikalnā, q.v.
v.n. To count the stars, to get no sleep, to be wakeful
